methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $100M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Ocean Infinity, a Growth-stage maritime technology company founded by Oliver Plunkett, has raised over $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a limited comparable universe: one identifiable private peer (Orbital Marine Power) and no public comps in the Ocean & Maritime sector, reflecting the nascent nature of this technology domain.

The stage-progression method provides the primary valuation signal. Using total capital raised ($100M) as a proxy baseline and applying a standard Growth-stage step-up multiplier of 1.2x yields an implied valuation of $120M. This approach assumes capital deployed correlates with value creation and that growth-stage financings typically command a 20% premium over cumulative investment.

The triangulation analysis supports a valuation range of $102M to $138M, with a median of $120M. The tight range ($102M–$138M) reflects reliance on a single applicable method rather than convergence across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include limited public and private comparables in the ocean technology sector, absence of disclosed revenue or unit economics, and incomplete market data on peer valuations. Any refinement of this range would require additional revenue multiples, DCF inputs, or broader peer transaction data from the maritime technology ecosystem.
